76: **Brazil**, level with one other team on 7 points after three group games, open the day's action at 10:00 AM PT against **Japan**, who finished 2nd in **Group F** on 5 points. **Japan** have reached the knockout rounds four times but have never won a knockout match — losing to Turkey in 2002, Paraguay on penalties in 2010, Belgium in 2018, and Croatia on penalties in 2022. Beating the five-time champions would be a historic first. **Brazil** have won 11 of 14 all-time meetings, but **Japan** stunned them 3-2 in an October 2025 friendly — rallying from 2-0 down for their first-ever win over the Seleção. Vinícius Jr has four goals so far, all in back-to-back 3-0 group wins. **Brazil**'s goal-difference edge of +6 underlines their attacking threat, though **Japan**'s own +4 suggests this is no pushover — the Asian side have the tactical discipline and set-piece quality to test any defence.
74: **Germany**, level with one other team on 6 points heading into the knockouts, meet **Paraguay**, also unable to be separated from a fellow side after finishing level on 4 points. **Germany** beat **Paraguay** 1-0 in their only previous tournament meeting, at the 2002 group stage. **Germany**'s 10 group-stage goals came from seven different scorers — the most balanced attack in the competition — with substitute Deniz Undav netting three without starting a match. Both teams have questions to answer: **Germany** must prove their point-share doesn't mask defensive vulnerabilities, while **Paraguay** — negative on goal difference — need to show they can boss a game rather than merely survive one. A tight, tactical affair looks inevitable at 1:30 PM PT.
75: The evening showpiece at 6:00 PM PT sends **Netherlands**, the outright winners of **Group F** on 7 points, against a **Morocco** side that is level with one other team on 7 points, their exact position in their own group not yet resolved. In a remarkable twist, this match falls exactly 32 years to the day since **Netherlands** beat **Morocco** 2-1 at USA '94 — also in a tournament on American soil. Current Dutch coach Ronald Koeman started that 1994 match. **Morocco**'s 2022 semi-final run was the best finish by an African nation in tournament history; the **Netherlands** are three-time finalists who have never won it. Both arrive with identical points hauls but contrasting narratives — **Netherlands** dominant going forward, **Morocco** built on defensive solidity and lethal transitions. This one has the quality and the stakes to define the entire round.
73: History beckons for both sides: **South Africa**, sitting 2nd in **Group A** on 4 points, face **Canada**, who are level with one other team on 4 points, their position in their own group not yet separated — making this a genuine meeting of equals before a ball is kicked. Neither nation has ever won a knockout tie at this stage of the finals — and the two sides have met only once before, a 2-0 **South Africa** friendly win in 2007, making this their first competitive encounter. **South Africa** never advanced past the group stage before this tournament, including as 2010 hosts. With Alphonso Davies fit for **Canada** and Teboho Mokoena returning for **South Africa**, the midfield battle could settle it — and extra time and penalties are waiting if 90 minutes aren't enough.
